content:
The Alumni Association of Jai Hind College is registered as a separate entity & is governed by a committee comprising of a Managing Board member, Principal, Management representative & illustrious Alumni. The committee is headed by the President & the composition of the committee can be changed periodically. The day to day functioning is taken care of by the Secretary of the Association. 
The main objective of the Alumni Association is to support the growth & development of the institution by raising funds , building a corpus & being a partner in the journey of excellence.
title: Alumni | Jai Hind College

content: The Alumni Association plays a pivotal role in fostering a strong bond between the institution and its graduates. With a wide range of activities, the association ensures that the alumni remain connected to their alma mater and contribute to its growth and development. One of the most anticipated events organized by the association is the Annual Alumni Reunion, which brings together graduates from different batches, creating a platform for networking and reminiscing about their college days. Jai Hind College is beaming with pride as we celebrate 75 years of nurturing minds and shaping futures.To honor this special occasion, we hosted our largest annual alumni meet, a heartwarming gathering
title: Alumni | Jai Hind College

It was a joyous celebration of our shared history, filled with stories of success and camaraderie. This event rekindled old friendships and inspired new dreams, reflecting the enduring spirit of our beloved college.

With a keen focus on revitalizing the startup economy, our goal is to bolster the nation’s economic growth. They offer:– Personalized mentoring by industry experts– Modern co-working spaces– Specialized programs to develop entrepreneurial skills– Opportunities to pitch to investorsTheir flagship event, “The Startup Exposition,” offers a platform for startups to showcase their ideas and connect with potential investors. “Intellectus” sessions educate startups on intellectual property rights, while their collaboration with the “Wadhwani Foundation” offers comprehensive support through online and offline programs.

content: Enactus Jai Hind College, part of the global Enactus network, uses entrepreneurship for social and environmental good. This initiative offers a chance to make a positive societal impact and helps develop a well-rounded skill set, fostering creativity and emotional intelligence.
title: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College

content:
Project Green Tee, is a quest born from the fraught uncertainties bestowed by the pandemic, to impart opportunities for alternative employment, financial independence, and personal empowerment, to women trudging in fragile hope through the landscape of some parts of Mumbai’s.
This initiative served two purpose – humanity and sustainability . It featured the participation of this marginalized community to recycle single-use plastic bottles into hand-sewn, stylish recycled t-shirt garments fit for everyday use.
title: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College

content: We tackled a mass scale outreach effort to obtain plastic bottles from personal sources to external ones the likes of popular hotel chains. These bottles were then further segregated according to color, with their caps and labels removed, and shredded into small colorful chips. Following a warm rinse and trip to the ovens, the flakes were melted into tiny pellets, undergoing stringent filtration and contamination removal. The filaments are combined and winded into stronger threads to create weighted spools of yarn. The yarn is subsequently stretched, carded, and roved before it is spun into fabric and is sent to our beneficiaries workplace and then the fabric is sewn into our Green – Tees
title: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College

content: fabric and is sent to our beneficiaries workplace and then the fabric is sewn into our Green – Tees by our beneficiaries . And this is how 7 PET bottles can be transformed into 1 – uber comfortable, stylish t – shirt. Our team of over 120 volunteers were trained to promote sales within their networks.We reached out to fashion influencers and thrift stores for brand visibility.GreenTee has also garnered a substantial instagram presence of 800+ followers upon its launch.We have impacted an audience of 900+ people through seminars and upskilling workshops on sustainable practices, designed to draw attention to our project.
title: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College

content: Project Tech Shiksha, launched in December of 2020, was inclined towards slightly different goals. The project served to impart holistic learning to the under-privileged and poverty-ridden youth, whose education had come to a still-stop due to Coronavirus concerns. A significantly huge band of volunteers, with a count spanning in increasing double digits, eagerly volunteered to carry the initiative forward, through customised curriculums conducted over several online classes. In this vein, two distinct sessions on the terrors of plastic waste, as well as the necessity of sustainability, were conducted with the kids involved in the project.
title: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College

content:
* 120+ kids and women directly impacted and educated via upskilling workshops and educational classes
  * Conducted one-on-one classes in technical subjects such as math and science, as well as stimulating workshops in arts and crafts for more than 50 underprivileged children and students who lost access to education as a result of the pandemic.
title: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College

content: Jai Hind College strictly prohibits sexual harassment in accordance with the Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition, and Redressal) Act, 2013. Sexual harassment is a punishable offense. The college has established an Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) to address any issues related to sexual harassment.
title: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College

content: Jai Hind College represented India at the HSBC/HKU Asia Pacific Business Case Competition 2017 in Hong Kong. The team, including Upamanyu Acharya, Diveek Jain, Pratik Malhotra, and Ishaan Singh, along with Faculty Advisor Dr. Rakhi Sharma, reached the quarter finals, becoming a Top 16 team globally. Representing India, which had one of the most competitive national competitions of any of the teams in Asia Pacific, Jai Hind College is proud of their achievement and hopes to build on our legacy to help next years’ teams progress even further.
title: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College

content:
The Case Study Cohort focuses on training students to adopt a business mindset and solve real-world cases analytically. Sessions include insights from mentors, faculty, and industry specialists, promoting hands-on business discussions and solution generation.

content: All political science students are members of this association.The Political Science Forum organizes events and talks to inform students about important national and international issues. In the 2014-15 academic year, the forum hosted a guest lecture on ‘Human Rights’ by Dr. Rashmi Oza and a talk on the “Socio-Political & Economic Dimensions of Globalization” by Mr. Arun Ferera. The popular ‘Youth Parliament’ event provided students with insights into parliamentary proceedings. Ten of our students were selected by Mumbai University for the state-level competition.

content: The Sexual Harassment Prohibition Committee aims to prevent sexual harassment of women employees at the workplace and is mandatory for all colleges. The committee’s inaugural event, “Question Everything,” was held in collaboration with “Laadli” including several short film screenings and a group discussion led by renowned singer Miss Suneeta Rao. Additionally, they also organized a poster competition named “Splash,” focusing on gender issues, with participation from students across various courses.

content: Jai Hind College joined the University of Mumbai’s Lifelong Learning and Extension Unit in 2009-10. The extension cell aims to sensitize students to social issues through community projects like women’s status surveys, population education, the Annapurna food sales project, and career exhibitions. Students earn 10 marks and a certificate for 120 hours of community work.

content: Throughout the years, Jai Hind College’s Nature Club has been actively engaging students in various environmental initiatives. Beginning with a memorable Nature Trail to Uttan in December 2013, accompanied by Mr. Udhav, Ms. Leena, and Dr. Devangi, the club sparked enthusiasm among commerce students for nature observation. In February 2014, a screening of the BBC documentary “Broken Tail” raised awareness about tiger conservation, emphasizing their crucial role in ecosystems. Further activities included a talk on environmental pollution and plastics in August 2014, a tree plantation drive in collaboration with CSR and NSS, and a sale of medicinal plants in September. The club’s upcoming
title: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College

title: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College

title: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College

title: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College

Wellness Cell serves as a platform for creating a safe, non-judgmental space for self-expression and emotional management
It is dedicated to promoting health and well-being amongst staff and students of Jai Hind College.

content: The Dot Com Club organized an outstanding tech fest this academic year, Cyberstrike 2025, with the theme “Genesis and Exodus.” The event was a perfect combination of technical, gaming, and cultural activities that attracted participants and audiences alike. The fest featured thrilling gaming competitions, including popular titles like Valorant, FIFA, BGMI, and VR games, offering an exciting platform for gamers to showcase their skills. On the cultural side, there were engaging events such as a ramp walk, canvas painting sessions, and an intriguing escape room that added a creative and interactive element to the fest.
title: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College

content: In addition to the fest, the club also demonstrated its commitment to social responsibility through E-Waste 2024, a CSR initiative that aimed to raise awareness about electronic waste management. As part of this initiative, the club successfully collected an impressive 454 kg of e-waste from institutes across South Bombay. The activity was further enriched by informative seminars and conferences, providing valuable insights into the importance of sustainable practices and environmental conservation. The combination of innovative events and meaningful initiatives made this year’s activities by the Dot Com Club truly remarkable.
title: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College

content: Entourage is the premier intercollegiate financial festival of Jai Hind College’s Department of BAF, BBI, and BFM. True to its name, Entourage brings together the brightest financial and creative minds for two days of intense competition, strategic thinking, and corporate exposure. With engaging pre-events and a decade-long legacy, the fest challenges participants to navigate real-world financial complexities while showcasing their innovation, problem-solving skills, and cultural talents. More than just a competition, Entourage is an exciting platform for learning, networking, and pushing boundaries in a dynamic and electrifying environment.
title: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College

content: The Family Managed Business Hub provides a centralized platform for collaboration, knowledge-sharing, and networking among family-owned enterprises. It serves as a supportive ecosystem where these businesses can address common challenges, exchange insights, and foster synergies. Such a hub facilitates the continuity and growth of family businesses by offering resources, mentorship, and a sense of community. Additionally, it contributes to the preservation of family values and legacies, creating a robust network for mutual support and shared success.
title: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College

content:
To encourage mutual respect among students, teachers, and staff regardless of background, fostering a safe and inclusive atmosphere, creating a focused learning environment thus maintaining the dignity and public image of the institution through appropriate conduct of its members.
  * Students shall wear their identity cards at all times while present on college premises.
  * The University of Mumbai mandates a minimum of 75% attendance in each semester to be eligible for examinations; however, the college expects students to maintain attendance levels as high as possible beyond this requirement.
title: Code of conduct | Jai Hind College

content:
* The use of mobile phones within college premises— classrooms, libraries, laboratories, corridors—is strictly prohibited unless permitted under a teacher’s supervision. 
  * During examinations, mobile phones must be switched off and kept aside to prevent any unfair practices. 
  * Maintaining discipline across the campus is expected of every student.
  * Any damage to college property will result in a monetary fine.
  * Ragging in any form is strictly prohibited on campus and is considered an anti-social and criminal act, liable to rustication and legal proceedings.
title: Code of conduct | Jai Hind College

content: * Students are not permitted to bring outsiders onto the campus without prior written permission from the college authorities.
title: Code of conduct | Jai Hind College

content:
The following activities are prohibited and will invite strict disciplinary action
  * Any activity carried out without proper authorization that goes against the ethos of the college or harms its reputation.
  * Disrespect, unruly behavior, use of harsh words and defiance shown towards teaching, non-teaching and/or administrative personnel. Threatening to physically harm or physically harming a fellow student, teaching or non-teaching staff 
  * Discriminatory remarks on grounds of gender, race, religion, and caste.
  * Any form of physical coercion and/or unwelcomed sexual behavior or taking sexual advantage of another person without consent
title: Code of conduct | Jai Hind College

content: * The use of mobile phones on campus for recording lectures or conversations, taking photographs without consent, or playing loud mus
title: Code of conduct | Jai Hind College

content:
Teachers of this academic institution are subject to the guidelines founded by UGC for college teachers. As per the UGC directives, every college teacher assumes the responsibility to conduct oneself in accordance with the standards of this profession. 
  * Be committed towards the needs of students and sensitivity to satisfy the querent’s mind
  * Treat all students equally irrespective of academic performance, caste, class, religion, gender, and place of origin. Avoid any partiality or vindictive attitude towards learners
  * Conform to the ethos of the profession and act in a professional and dignified manner
title: Code of conduct | Jai Hind College

content:
* Teachers must update their knowledge and skills by attending various courses, workshops, conferences, seminars, etc. so that students, departments, and the institution benefit from their growth.
  * Besides teaching, teachers should participate in research activities and inculcate research attitude in students.
  * Teachers should adopt updated pedagogy and innovative methodology to keep abreast with changes in the global academic scenario.
  * Teachers shall complete administrative responsibilities entrusted to them besides carrying out extra-curricular and co-curricular programmes.
title: Code of conduct | Jai Hind College

content:
* There should be no conflict between professional, private, and personal practices such as avoiding taking tuitions in the concerned subject which might impact their quality of classroom teaching.
  * Information regarding all aspects of examination should strictly be kept confidential.
title: Code of conduct | Jai Hind College

content:
* Teachers should be respectful, courteous, and cooperative towards junior and senior colleagues.
  * Collective efforts should be taken to complete academic and administrative goals.
  * Teachers should refrain from making false allegations on colleagues for personal vendetta.
title: Code of conduct | Jai Hind College

content:
The code of conduct is also applicable to the non-teaching and support staff of the college.
  * Maintain punctuality and should not be under the influence of drugs or alcohol at work.
  * Familiarise and adhere to the rules and policies of the institution.
  * Be sincere and earnest towards the duties assigned to them and also help one another when required.
  * No other employment or financial commitment to be made outside college premises during work hours.
  * Maintain apolitical environment and take all internal issues to the Principal to sort out.
  * Communication between colleagues, students and teaching staff should be polite, avoiding harsh tone and words.
title: Code of conduct | Jai Hind College

content:
* Non-teaching staff should maintain confidentiality of records regarding administrative and examination matters.
  * When non-teaching staff are entrusted with confidential documents for transportation, photocopying or other responsibilities, they should be cautious not to leak this information.
  * Perform duties with integrity, veracity and without boundaries of discrimination of caste, class, religion and gender.
  * Non-teaching staff should behave in a professional and helpful manner in libraries, science laboratories, computer laboratories and administrative offices towards students and teaching staff.
title: Code of conduct | Jai Hind College

content: NASSCOM a not-for-profit industry association, is the apex body for the $227 billion dollar IT BPM industry in India, an industry that had made a phenomenal contribution to India’s GDP, exports, employment, infrastructure and global visibility. In India, this industry provides the highest employment in the private sector. Established in 1988 and ever since, NASSCOM‟s relentless pursuit has been to constantly support the IT BPM industry, in the latter‟s continued journey towards seeking trust and respect from varied stakeholders, even as it reorients itself time and again to remain innovative, without ever losing its humane and friendly touch. NASSCOM is focused on building the architecture
title: Collaborations | Jai Hind College

In turn, the IT BPM industry has gained recognition as a global powerhouse. content: is a Not-for-Profit Organization, registered under the Societies Registration Act, 1860, promoted by the Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) with inclusive representation of the Government, Industry, Industry Associations and Training Institutes across India. Formed by the Industry and for the industry to tackle the skilling of large manpower to fulfil the industry requirements, THSC plays a crucial role in bridging this ever-growing gap. We have a mandate to create a robust and sustainable eco-system for skill development in the industry, catering to all the sub sectors of the industry, namely, Hotels, Tour Operators, Food Service Restaurants, Facilities Management and Cruise Liners.
title: Collaborations | Jai Hind College

content: is an independent global think tank based in Delhi, India. The foundation has three centres in Mumbai, Chennai and Kolkata. ORF provides potentially viable inputs for policy and decision-makers in the Indian Government and to the political and business communities of India. Propelled by the process of reforms initiated in the 1990s, ORF, over the past 30 years of its existence, has effectively narrated and participated in India‟s story as the country has acquired an unmistakable global footprint. From primarily looking inward and engaging with domestic reforms, to gradually forging global partnerships, ORF today plays a seminal role in building political and policy consensus that enables
title: Collaborations | Jai Hind College

content: ORF today plays a seminal role in building political and policy consensus that enables India to interact with the world. ORF helps discover and inform India‟s choices. It carries Indian voices and ideas to forums shaping global debates. It provides non-partisan, independent, well-researched analyses and inputs to diverse decision- makers in governments, business communities, and academia and to civil society around the world. The mandate undertaken by them is to conduct in-depth research, provide inclusive platforms and invest in tomorrow‟s thought leaders today. ORF has been associated with us for last eight years on our International Relations Course.
title: Collaborations | Jai Hind College

content: The NMF was accordingly established, in 2005, as the nation‟s first maritime think-tank for the conduct of independent and policy- relevant research on all „matters maritime‟. It is a policy-relevant research organisation of acknowledged excellence that would not only undertake serious, cutting-edge maritime research of its own but would also provide a common platform for advocacy, discourse, and debate, between maritime-related national and international institutions, organisations, and academic establishments, as also reputed and renowned individuals, professionals, and practitioners the world over. Jai Hind College and NMF are associated by an MoU for our International Relations Course.
title: Collaborations | Jai Hind College

content: is the leading integrated travel services company in the country offering a broad spectrum of services that include Foreign Exchange, Corporate Travel, MICE, Leisure Travel, Value Added Services, Visa & Passport services and E-Business. The company set up its first office in India in 1881. TCIL has been felicitated with The Best Travel Agency – India at TTG Travel Awards 2019, The Best Outbound Tour Operator at the Times Travel Awards 2018 & 2019 and Leading Company with Cutting Edge Travel Innovation at the Times, Silver award for Asia’s Best Integrated Report (First Time) category at the Asia Sustainability Reporting Awards, Best Risk Management-Framework & Systems at the India Risk
title: Collaborations | Jai Hind College

content: Asia Sustainability Reporting Awards, Best Risk Management-Framework & Systems at the India Risk Management Awards 2019; Best Cash Management Solution – India at the Asset Triple A Treasury, Trade, Supply Chain & Risk Management Awards 2018, Best Outbound Tour Operator at the SATTE awards 2019, Excellence in Domestic Tour Operations at the SATTE Awards 2018, The French Ambassador‟s Award for Exemplary Achievements in Visa Issuance – 2015 to 2019 and the Condé Nast Traveller – Readers‟ Travel Awards from 2011 to 2019. Our partnership with Thomas cook is to enhance and hone the students‟ skills for the Tourism Industry and provide internships and placements opportunities to our students.
title: Collaborations | Jai Hind College

content: LLP Jai Hind College has recently signed an MoU with LCGC Chrom Consumables LLP on the 27th of May, 2022. Such a collaboration will help bridge the gap between Industry-Academia making students more employable and career-ready with the right skill-sets. Initially for a period of 2 years, the MoU aims at providing training to both under-graduate and postgraduate students on cutting-edge technology in the field of science through internships/workshops/seminars/lecture series conducted with the expertise of LCGC, pioneers in the field of chromatography and other techniques related to separation and purification of molecules.
title: Collaborations | Jai Hind College

content:
I welcome you, and extend my warmest greetings from the Principal’s desk at Jai Hind College.
We, at Jai Hind College, are committed to providing you with a comprehensive and enriching educational experience. Our dedicated faculty has explored a wide range of subjects, ensuring that you receive a holistic education that prepares you for success in all aspects of life.
title: From The Principal’s Desk | Jai Hind College

content: Education is the key to unlocking one’s true potential, as the famous Irish poet, William Butler Yeats once said, “Education is not the filling of a pail, but the lighting of a fire”. We firmly imbibe this principle in our teaching methodologies. Our faculty members are passionate about imparting knowledge that empowers students such as you to make a positive impact on society. Through interactive lectures, practical sessions, and collaborative projects, we aim to foster critical thinking, creativity, and problem-solving skills in all our students.
title: From The Principal’s Desk | Jai Hind College

content: The recently introduced National Education Policy (NEP) has brought about significant changes in the Indian education system by focusing on holistic development, multidisciplinary learning, and skill enhancement. At Jai Hind College, we have embraced NEP’s principles, and are dedicated to nurturing well-rounded individuals who possess not only subject knowledge but also the ability to adapt, innovate, and contribute meaningfully to society.
title: From The Principal’s Desk | Jai Hind College

content:
I am delighted to share that Jai Hind College has been granted “Empowered Autonomous College” status by the University of Mumbai in 2023. This recognition symbolizes our commitment to academic excellence and gives us the freedom to design innovative programs and curricula that align with the needs of the industry and society, and provide our students with a competitive edge.
As we continue on this journey of excellence, we remain grateful for the unwavering support from all our stakeholders. Together, we will strive to nurture and empower the next generation of leaders, innovators, and change-makers.
title: From The Principal’s Desk | Jai Hind College

content:
In conclusion, I would like to quote Nelson Mandela’s views on education: “Education is the most powerful weapon.” Let us harness the power of education to create a better future, not only for ourselves but for the entire community. Together, we can make a difference.
Thank you for your continued trust in Jai Hind College.
title: From The Principal’s Desk | Jai Hind College

content: The Junior College section of is a dynamic multidisciplinary institution offering quality education in the fields of Science, Commerce, and Arts. Aided by the Government of Maharashtra, the college has consistently nurtured academic excellence and holistic development among students.
title: Junior College | Jai Hind College

content:
Over the years, the institution has built a strong legacy of outstanding performance in board examinations, with students progressing to successful careers in medicine, engineering, business, journalism, and several other professional fields. The Junior College is guided by a dedicated and experienced faculty that fosters curiosity, critical thinking, and personal growth. Through continuous mentorship and meaningful student-teacher interaction, the college creates a supportive environment where young minds are encouraged to achieve their fullest potential.
A student enters the college after passing the std X exams and is considered a student of junior college for 2 years.
title: Junior College | Jai Hind College

content:
A student studies 8 subjects and appears for 4 exams through the year in Std XI. On successful completion of the exams the student is promoted to std XII. In Std XII a student again studies 8 subjects and at the end of the year appears for a board exam conducted by the state of Maharashtra.  
|
title: Junior College | Jai Hind College

content:
|  
| --- |  
 |  
**COMPUTER SCIENCE & ELECTRONICS SUBJECT ALLOCATION ARE MERIT BASED AND CONSISTS OF 2 PAPERS.**
STD XI: In std XI a student appears for 4 exams.
**A student needs to score 35% marks to pass std XI.**
**STD XII: In std XII a student appears for 2 exams in college and finally a board exam conducted by the Maharashtra State Board.**
title: Junior College | Jai Hind College

content:
has always believed in the holistic development of its students. Beyond academics, the Junior College provides numerous opportunities for students to enhance their skills, discover their talents, and build confident, well-rounded personalities through a wide range of events, cultural activities, competitions, and co-curricular initiatives. These experiences encourage leadership, creativity, teamwork, and personal growth, helping students develop into capable and responsible individuals.
Students are given the opportunity to participate in all activities of the college. In addition, there are certain activities or societies exclusive to junior college students.
title: Junior College | Jai Hind College

content:
A non – judgmental, non – competitive platform for students to express themselves through any medium that they choose.
A society for Commerce students which conducts various activities through the year.
A club for science students to encourage learning maths and science through fun activities and relating it to daily life.
title: Junior College | Jai Hind College

content: Jai Hind College, Empowered Autonomous, Mumbai, has a history spanning several years since it’s inception in 1948. Soon after the Indian Independence, a group of visionary professors of D.J. Sind College, Karachi, and other eminent educationists founded Jai Hind College under the registered name of ‘Sind Educationists’ Association’ primarily for the educational rehabilitation of the Sindhi community. Their goal was to contribute their expertise, knowledge, and skills in order to set up an educational institution par excellence.
title: Legacy | Jai Hind College

content: From a humble Arts college, Jai Hind College soon diversified into Science in 1949, followed by Commerce in 1980. Post 2000, several Self-financed and Vocational Programs were introduced to improve employability of students, such as Bachelor of Management Studies (BMS), Bachelor of Mass Media (BMM), Bachelor of Accounting and Finance (BAF), Bachelor of Banking and Insurance (BBI), Bachelor of Financial Market (BFM) and, Bachelor of Vocational Studies in Travel & Tourism Management (B. Voc. TTM) and Software Development (B. Voc. SD).Programs such as Bachelor of Digital Strategy (BDS) , Master of Science in Big Data Analytics and Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) in collaboration with
title: Legacy | Jai Hind College

content: Science in Big Data Analytics and Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) in collaboration with TCS as the Industry partner,Bachelor In International Accounting (BIA) And Bachelor In Actuarial Science And Quantitative Finance (BAQF) were recently introduced. Inclusion of such Industry aligned Programs are periodically assessed based on feedback from all stakeholders to help align academics with current market demands.
title: Legacy | Jai Hind College

content:
Our notable alumni, such as Mr. Sunil Dutt,Mr.Piyush Goyal,Ms.Supriya Sule,Dr.Raghunath Mashelkar,Mr.Vikram Nankani,Mr Ajay Piramal, Ms Priyanka Chopra Jonas, Mr. John Abraham ,Mr. Vishal Dadlani, Mr.Shaan and many others began their illustrious journeys at Jai Hind College, proving that students can achieve national and global recognition with our foundation.
The Union Ministry of HRD and the University of Mumbai conferred on us the prestigious Empowered Autonomous Status for a period of ten years from the academic year 2023-24.
Over the years , Jai Hind College has empowered students to achieve their dreams while staying true to the value systems of the Founding Fathers.
title: Legacy | Jai Hind College

content:
The history of Jai Hind College library dates back to 1948 with the inception of Jai Hind College. The library was named in 1969 as the “T.M. Advani Memorial Library” in loving memory of the late Principal T.M. Advani.
The library has a rich collection of books consisting of a wide variety and subscribes to daily journals, periodicals, and magazines. The library also has a comprehensive reference collection of encyclopedias, handbooks, dictionaries, atlases, etc.
title: Library | Jai Hind College

content: The Dewey decimal classification system is followed to classify books in the library. The library has provided Open Access Facility since the 90s. It has a wide range of novels in different languages and other light reading materials catering to diverse interests. The library collection includes languages such as English, Marathi, Hindi, Sindhi, Gujarati, Sanskrit, and French.
title: Library | Jai Hind College

content: The library has a Reading Room Committee to support the functioning of the library and facilitate its development plans by advocating library development activities. The committee’s structure is as follows: Chairperson – Librarian; Members – teaching staff members from different streams and students. The purpose of the Reading Room Committee is to act as a channel of communication and dialogue between the library and its users.
title: Library | Jai Hind College

content: The library uses SLIM21 software and is a fully computerized library with online functional modules like book acquisition, circulation, catalog, serials, etc. The E-Library consists of e-journals, online access to the subscribed print journals, theses, teachers’ publications, lectures, seminar/conference proceedings, links of open access e-books suggested by teachers, in-house publications, etc. The E-Library is accessible on the intranet. The library provides free access to its members.
title: Library | Jai Hind College

content:
The library provides access to NLIST. All e-resources subscribed for the college under the NLIST project are now accessible to the library through the NLIST website. Remote access to these resources includes databases, e-journals, and e-books accessible to faculty, students, and researchers free of cost. Library members have to register to avail the N List personal user ID and password. The registration link for this membership is provided separately. Accessing and downloading articles is possible directly from the publishers’ websites. Jai Hind Library has been featured in the Top 10 users list of NLIST.
The College library was named the Principal T.M. Advani Memorial library
title: Library | Jai Hind College

content:
The College library was named the Principal T.M. Advani Memorial library
  * Virtual Book Displays with Open access e book link / N-List books
title: Library | Jai Hind College

content: The Central Instrumentation Facility (CIF) at Jai Hind College, inaugurated on 14th July 2015, is a state-of-the-art laboratory housing sophisticated analytical instruments designed to support advanced scientific research. These instruments have been procured through generous funding from DST-FIST, UGC, and the college management. The facility is accessible to faculty and students of the college and is also extended to external researchers. It serves as a hub for student and faculty research, while also hosting hands-on training workshops and internship programs, fostering skill development and innovation.
title: Research | Jai Hind College

content:
The list of instruments housed in the Central Instrumentation Facility (CIF) is as follows:
1. Differential Scanning Calorimeter2. High-Performance Liquid Chromatography with VWD3. FTIR Spectrometer4. Spectrofluorimeter5. Uv-vis Spectrometer6. Rotary Evaporator7. Binocular Microscope with LED8. Refrigerated Centrifuge9. Gel Documentation10. Gel Rocker11. Fraction Collector12. Melting point apparatus13. Double distillation unit14. Karl Fischer Apparatus15. Overhead stirrer16. Magnetic stirrer with temperature sensor17. Weighing Balance (1mg and 0.1 mg)18. Bath Sonicator
title: Research | Jai Hind College

**Empowering Futures at Jai Hind College: Training & Placement Cell**
At Jai Hind College, our Training & Placement Cell is the nexus of skill development and career opportunities, providing unparalleled support to students. With the backing of our esteemed faculty, we connect students with top-tier internships and placements.
We aim to cultivate industry-ready professionals equipped with technical knowledge, soft skills, and confidence to excel in their careers.
– : Tailored workshops on CV building, communication, body language, and interview strategies. – : Hands-on experience with 80+ leading companies. – : Robust recruitment drives with over 100 prestigious companies.
title: Student Activity | Jai Hind College

content:
Our students thrive in industries such as: – EdTech – Financial Services – Entertainment – Consulting – Banking – Marketing and Advertising – Information Technology
At Jai Hind College, we are committed to fostering holistic development for our students through a comprehensive mentoring initiative. This initiative aims to guide and support our students in both their academic and professional journeys by pairing students with experienced faculty members and industry experts who provide personalized guidance. This mentorship is designed to help students navigate their educational paths, develop critical skills, and prepare for successful careers.
title: Student Activity | Jai Hind College

content:
* Conducted in a classroom setting, these sessions allow students to interact directly with their mentors. This personal interaction helps in building strong relationships and provides a conducive environment for detailed discussions and feedback.
  * Utilizing modern technology, virtual mentoring sessions are arranged to provide flexibility and accessibility. This mode ensures that mentoring is not limited by geographical constraints and allows for continuous support.
title: Student Activity | Jai Hind College

content:
* Each student receives tailored advice and feedback, helping them to identify and achieve their individual goals.
  * Industry experts share insights and real-world experiences, preparing students for the demands of the professional world.
  * Faculty mentors assist with academic planning, subject-specific challenges, and research opportunities.
  * Students can build a network of contacts in their field of interest, opening doors to internships, job opportunities, and collaborative projects.
title: Student Activity | Jai Hind College

content:
Our mentors are a blend of esteemed faculty members and distinguished industry professionals who bring a wealth of knowledge and experience, ensuring that students receive a well-rounded perspective.
Students interested in getting mentoring can contact the program coordinator, where mentorship pairings are made based on the student’s field of study, interests, and career aspirations.
title: Student Activity | Jai Hind College

content: At Jai Hind College, we believe that effective mentoring is critical to student success. We are committed to empowering students, helping them reach their greatest potential, and preparing them for the challenges and possibilities of the future. We have a demonstrated track record of achievement, with many students reaching significant milestones in their academic and professional careers.
title: Student Activity | Jai Hind College

content:
This committee serves as a platform for students to voice their grievances regarding any aspect of campus life, such as infrastructure issues, dissatisfaction with faculty, administrative inefficiencies, or other concerns. The committee ensures that student complaints are heard promptly, investigated thoroughly, and addressed through appropriate measures. It acts as a bridge between students and the administration, ensuring transparency, accountability, and a responsive campus environment that works to maintain student satisfaction and well-being.

title: Student Activity | Jai Hind College

2. Then the concerned Professor/ Faculty member will send the LOR to college email id (contactus@jaihindcollege.edu.in) mentioning the student’s email id so that the Admin office can send the payment link .

SWAYAM is a programme initiated by Government of India and is designed to achieve the three cardinal principles of Education Policy viz., access, equity and quality. The objective of this effort is to take the best teaching learning resources to all, including the most disadvantaged. NPTEL is one of the 9 national coordinators which offer MOOCs in diverse areas like Architecture & Planning, Education, Engineering & Technology, Humanities & Arts, Law, Management & Commerce, Math & Sciences. Students can take up MOOCs offered by NPTEL-SWAYAM to claim credits in their academic program at Jai Hind College Autonomous.

Step by Step Guide to complete your Admission for MSc Big Data Analytics. Part I: 
 
⮚ Step 1: Go to the Jai Hind College Admissions portal: 
https://datavista.in/cms/student/Ext/auth/login_pg_SEP.php?sectionmaster_Id=26 
⮚ Step 2: Register/Login with a valid  email id and submit your online  Application 
form by making a successful payment of form fees. You  shall get an application 
number here. 
⮚ Step 3: The Admissions Cell will go through your submitted form and  after 
successful verification shall enable the onli ne payment option  for you to pay the 
requisite College Fees. You will get an alert (via  email) informing the activation of 
the college fee payment option.
title: PDF Document

the college fee payment option. 
⮚ Step 4: Once you get the alert, you will now have to login to your  admissions 
account with the same email id and password with which  you submitted  your 
online application form.  
⮚ Step.5: Click ‘Pay Now’ and proceed towards  payment of college fees. Upon 
successful payment, you will be able to print the Fees Receipt.
